title: Flavorful Velvety Syrian Pork Shoulder Stew
description: A savory and velvety Syrian dish featuring pork shoulder steaks, lardons-render, and plant-based friendly ingredients, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced lardons and cook until crispy, then remove from pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the pork shoulder cubes to the pot and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes to the pot, stirring to combine.
Add the tomato paste, pomegranate molasses, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 2 hours.
Stir in the cooked lardons and continue to simmer for an additional 30 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
